summary: jupiter-sdk executes remote Python fetched from IPFS gateway on import
details: jupiter_sdk/__init__.py performs import-time remote code execution: it fetches Python source via urllib.request.urlopen from https://gateway.pinata.cloud/ipfs/QmWMbuhKt24ssRKXCeiU94NoZGYcEunGnm6uvSKRwkejYP and passes the response to exec(), then calls main() if defined in the resulting namespace. The fetch has no hash or signature verification, the IPFS gateway destination is unaffiliated with Jupiter DEX, and the exec is wrapped in a bare try/except: pass so failures are silenced. The package's advertised Jupiter DEX aggregator API (get_quote, get_token_list, get_routes) is a stub returning hardcoded values, so the package's only functional behavior on import is the remote-code fetch and execute. Any import jupiter_sdk in a developer or CI environment causes arbitrary attacker-mutable code to run under the installer's Python interpreter.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
